comment: Chain-level harness rule fixture (P2-06). Each case is a compiled-chain fragment using -- begin_script:/-- end_script: markers; `expected` is the ordered [script_name, index, statement] tuples a runner must produce. The rule: at each begin_script the active terminator resets to ';' and the per-script emitted-statement index restarts at 1, so statement_id <script>:<index> is identical to running the per-script files and terminator state never leaks across scripts. SET TERM directives are consumed and not counted. Reference: drivers/driver/python/src/scratchbird/sql.py::iter_chain_statements.

cases.name: terminator_does_not_leak_across_scripts
expected: CREATE PROCEDURE p AS BEGIN x = 1; END

cases.name: index_restarts_per_script
set_term_not_counted_in_index
expected: CREATE PROCEDURE p AS BEGIN a = 1; END

cases.name: chain_header_outside_markers_ignored
cases.input:
-- ScratchBird driver full-surface SBSQL chain
-- namespace: sbtest_ns

-- begin_script: only.sbsql
-- script_id: SBDFS-999
SELECT 42;
-- end_script: only.sbsql
